# UC-22.59.11 · Shore-to-ship satcom remote-access governance — VSAT / Iridium Certus / Inmarsat-FleetBroadband PAM enforcement with WYSIWYS for OT-changes

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Ships are visited by remote engineers from shore — they fix problems over satellite without coming on board. We make sure every one of these remote visits goes through a controlled gateway, that the engineer has logged in properly, the session is recorded, and the engineer signs off on what they did.*

---

## Description

Inspects every shore-to-ship remote-access session over satcom; verifies PAM mediation, MFA presence, session recording, and WYSIWYS confirmation for OT-system changes; raises a notable on any governance gap.

## Value

OEM remote-support sessions and shore-IT remote-admin sessions are the highest-risk daily activities on a connected fleet. The 2019 'Marine Money' analysis estimated 35% of fleet cyber-incidents had a remote-session as the entry vector. Without PAM + WYSIWYS, a single OEM-engineer mistake or compromised endpoint can affect dozens of vessels at once. This UC closes the gap with continuous evidence.

## Implementation

(1) Deploy CyberArk PSM (or equivalent — BeyondTrust, Senhasegura, Delinea) on the shore-side network with reverse-proxy mediation for every fleet OT system. (2) Implement WYSIWYS workflow: for any OT-config change, the OEM engineer signs an attestation in PSM specifying the intended change and the vessel ID before commit. (3) Schedule UC-22.59.11 hourly; route at severity P1 for any governance gap on OT zones.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.59.11` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `imo_clause="BIMCO-Cyber-satcom"`. Every row pins (imo_number, session_id, source_user, source_company, target_vessel, target_zone, target_system, satcom_path, mfa_present, session_recorded, wysiwys_confirmed, governance_indicator).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

An OEM engineer connects directly to a vessel ECDIS through VSAT without going through the PAM gateway. UC-22.59.11 MUST flag governance_indicator=true within 1 hour with mfa_present=false and session_recorded=false.

### Negative scenario

OEM engineer connects via PAM gateway with MFA, session recording, and WYSIWYS confirmation for an OT-config change. UC-22.59.11 MUST record governance_indicator=false.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.59.4 in production.
- A PAM solution (CyberArk PSM, BeyondTrust PRA, Senhasegura, Delinea) deployed shore-side with reverse-proxy mediation for every fleet OT system.
- Onboard firewall configured to deny direct satcom-to-OT traffic; only the PAM gateway is allowed to initiate.

## Step 1 — Satcom-session KV Store

```ini
[imo_satcom_session_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.session_id = string
field.imo_number = string
field.source_user = string
field.source_company = string
field.target_zone = string
field.target_system = string
field.satcom_path = string
field.wysiwys_confirmed = string
field.wysiwys_signed_by = string
field.wysiwys_signed_at = time
field.intent_description = string
field.approved_by = string
field.session_start = time
field.session_end = time
```

## Step 2 — WYSIWYS workflow

Before committing any OT change, the PAM session prompts the engineer with a structured form: vessel ID, target system, intended change description, change ticket reference, sign-off. The form is hashed and the hash + signature are written to the KV Store.

## Step 3 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.59.11: IMO Satcom Remote-Access Governance]
cron_schedule = 35 * * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.59.11,reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=BIMCO-Cyber-satcom
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 4 — Runbooks

**`governance_indicator=true` on target_zone in [ibs, ecdis, gmdss, ais, propulsion, dps]** (owner: CISO + DPA, severity P1): Within 1 hour, terminate any in-progress session; investigate; document for the next DoC verification.

## Step 5 — Maritime-specific notes

- **Bandwidth-sensitive operation**: PAM session recording can be heavy on Iridium-only operation; the offline-replay variant captures locally and uploads at port-call.
- **OEM-cloud direct connections**: when OEMs use their own cloud platforms (Wartsila Online Services, ABB Marine OneView, Kongsberg CloudPort) that side-step the PAM gateway, that's a governance gap; either bring them into PAM or accept the documented exception.

## SPL

```spl
(sourcetype="cyberark:session" OR sourcetype="cyberark:audit" OR sourcetype="pan:traffic" OR sourcetype="fortinet:fortigate:traffic" OR sourcetype="iridium:certus:audit" OR sourcetype="inmarsat:fbb:audit")
    satcom_path IN ("vsat","iridium-certus","inmarsat-fleetbroadband","inmarsat-c")
    target_zone IN ("ibs","ins","gmdss","ais","ecdis","propulsion","cargo-control")
| eval session_recorded = case(sourcetype = "cyberark:session", "true", session_via_pam = "true", "true", 1=1, "false")
| eval mfa_present = case(auth_factors >= 2, "true", 1=1, "false")
| lookup imo_satcom_session_lookup session_id OUTPUT wysiwys_confirmed wysiwys_signed_by wysiwys_signed_at
| eval governance_indicator = case(
    session_recorded = "false", "true",
    mfa_present = "false", "true",
    isnull(wysiwys_confirmed) OR wysiwys_confirmed = "false", "true",
    1=1, "false")
| where governance_indicator = "true"
| table _time imo_number session_id source_user source_company target_vessel target_zone target_system satcom_path mfa_present session_recorded wysiwys_confirmed governance_indicator
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Open governance gaps (24h). (Panel 2) Per-vessel session distribution by satcom path. (Panel 3) Top OEM companies by session volume. (Panel 4) WYSIWYS attestation cadence.

## Known False Positives

1. **Iridium-only vessel low-bandwidth**: PAM session recording may be skipped on Iridium-only operation due to bandwidth; allow with `vessel_satcom_class=iridium-only` AND `session_replay_offline=true` (the session is recorded ashore and replayed on the next port-call). 2. **Emergency-distress comms via GMDSS**: GMDSS distress comms are exempt. 3. **Iridium-Certus tunnel maintenance**: scheduled satcom-tunnel maintenance windows.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `imo_22_59_11_exceptions` with the CISO + DPA's documented approval.

## References

- [BIMCO Guidelines on Cyber Security Onboard Ships — Satcom and remote access](https://www.bimco.org/about-us-and-our-members/publications/the-guidelines-on-cyber-security-onboard-ships)
- [IACS UR E26 — Cyber resilience of ships, access control](https://iacs.org.uk/publications/unified-requirements/ur-e/)
- [IMO MSC-FAL.1/Circ.3 Rev.2 — Communication systems](https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/OurWork/Security/Documents/MSC-FAL.1-Circ.3-Rev.2.pdf)
